narrative.player_intent: Delay notification for a stronger evidence base.
narrative.dramatic_role: The road that lets exposure and the press cycle catch up.

routes.label: Loop in comms before deciding
routes.stakes: Bringing comms in early shapes what gets said before the decision is even made, at a small cost in goodwill — a proper process now is cheaper than a fight later.
routes.hook: The line to take is being drafted whether or not you've decided what the line is.

narrative.player_intent: Involve comms before committing to a disclosure path.
narrative.dramatic_role: Route into the statement-drafting branch.

routes.label: Keep working through the night instead of resting
routes.stakes: Another pass through the logs without sleep might catch what you missed, at the cost of a third of a day. It might also be the pass that breaks you.
routes.hook: Nobody is going to tell you to stop. That's exactly the problem.

narrative.player_intent: Push past exhaustion for another shot at the evidence.
narrative.dramatic_role: The optional, repeatable risk that can end the run outright.

routes.label: Get a verified backup status, not an assumed one
routes.stakes: Two hours to stop assuming the backups are clean and actually find out — the architecture diagram says they're segmented from the rest of the network. Diagrams are aspirational documents.
routes.hook: Someone finally asks the technical team the question everyone's been avoiding: have you actually checked, or are we all just hoping?

narrative.player_intent: Verify recovery capability before it becomes the deciding factor.
narrative.dramatic_role: Grants backup_status_report, which gates m080 in place of the earlier hours-remaining threshold — restoration viability now depends on evidence obtained, not on how much of the attacker's countdown happens to be left.

routes.label: Notify the ICO now, after the wait for certainty
routes.stakes: You're comfortably inside the seventy-two hours — UK GDPR's 'without undue delay' test is about whether the wait was justified, not just about the outer clock, and a wait spent on evidence-gathering is a defensible one. It's still worth being able to explain why you didn't report on partial evidence sooner, the way the ICO's own guidance actually recommends.
routes.hook: The certainty took longer than it needed to. Notify now, and be ready to explain the wait, not apologise for missing a deadline you didn't miss.

narrative.player_intent: Notify the ICO after choosing to wait for certainty first.
narrative.dramatic_role: Flags the delay as a judgement call worth reviewing, not a compliance breach — ico_notification_delayed is deliberately not a 'lateness' flag; the statutory 72-hour clock was never actually at risk on any reachable path into this route.

routes.label: Hold the line on silence a while longer
routes.stakes: Silence held past the point of plausibility stops looking like caution, and costs another day you don't have.
routes.hook: The reporter's questions aren't going away because you don't answer them.

narrative.player_intent: Continue withholding disclosure.
narrative.dramatic_role: The road into the cover-up ending.

routes.label: Issue the statement as drafted
routes.stakes: The fuller draft goes out, as written, with everything currently known about scope and impact.
routes.hook: Legal signed off. It just didn't sign off enthusiastically.

narrative.player_intent: Publish without further amendment.
narrative.dramatic_role: The direct route out of comms_prep — labelled neutrally on purpose; the reader shouldn't be told this is 'the honest one' before they choose, only afterwards, by consequence.

routes.label: Hold for exec sign-off first
routes.stakes: Sign-off is procedure. It is also an opportunity for the statement to change hands, and six hours — and a little more goodwill — you don't get back.
routes.hook: The Leader's office has asked to see it before it goes anywhere.

narrative.player_intent: Route the statement through executive approval.
narrative.dramatic_role: Route into the political pressure encounter.

routes.label: Issue the current statement now
routes.foreshadow: Holding this line in front of the Leader takes political capital, not just conviction — and if you already spent most of yours forcing an earlier argument, you won't have enough left to win this one.
routes.stakes: The statement goes out as drafted, on the timetable you already had — no further sign-off, no further review, whatever's in it is what residents and press get.
routes.hook: The Leader wants another look at the wording. You have the wording you have.

narrative.player_intent: Decline the Leader's request to revisit the wording.
narrative.dramatic_role: Gated on comms_capital, so the reckless early-reopening road forecloses it while the careful, comms-led road preserves it. Deliberately labelled without moral framing — the reader shouldn't be told in the menu that this is the 'right' choice, only shown the consequence afterwards.

routes.label: Accept the Leader's office revisions before publishing
routes.stakes: The revised version goes out under time pressure, without a line-by-line check against what was cut and why — a reasonable-sounding request to smooth the tone can, in practice, remove exactly the sentence that mattered.
routes.hook: The redline comes back fast. Nobody in the room has time to compare it word for word against the version legal signed off.

narrative.player_intent: Accept edits from the Leader's office under time pressure.
narrative.dramatic_role: The road into the cover-up ending — deliberately not labelled as capitulation; the risk here is process (unreviewed edits under pressure), and the outcome should surprise a player who thought they were just accepting a reasonable request.

routes.label: Issue a short holding statement now; publish the fuller account once affected records are confirmed
routes.stakes: A short, accurate statement of what's confirmed goes out now — an incident is under investigation, services are affected, more detail follows as it's verified — with the fuller account to come once the affected-records question is actually answered rather than guessed at.
routes.hook: It satisfies nobody completely. The Leader gets the caution about unconfirmed numbers; residents get told something is happening, tonight, not next week.

narrative.player_intent: Split the difference between speed and certainty rather than choosing one.
narrative.dramatic_role: The third, genuinely neutral political_pressure exit — the same phased-disclosure principle the ICO itself recommends (report what's confirmed, update as the picture develops), applied to public comms instead of the regulator. Not free: costs 6 hours, same as any other delay route.

routes.label: Loop in the council's cyber insurer before deciding on the ransom
routes.stakes: The policy has an opinion on this decision whether or not you ask for it; better to hear it before you choose.
routes.hook: Someone in finance has already found the incident-response hotline number in the policy documents.

narrative.player_intent: Consult the insurer before the ransom decision.
narrative.dramatic_role: Hub spoke into the insurance-informed finale.

routes.label: Directly contact the residents whose data was actually taken
routes.stakes: A general press statement tells the town something happened. It doesn't tell the specific residents on the affected data schedule that their own data is out — and where a breach is likely to bring them real harm, the law expects them told directly, not left to wonder if the council statement was about them.
routes.hook: The general statement covers the council. It doesn't cover the people whose bank details just left the building.

narrative.player_intent: Meet the separate, individual-level notification duty a public statement doesn't discharge.
narrative.dramatic_role: Deliberately distinct from public_statement_made and regulator_notified: UK GDPR draws three separate lines here — tell the regulator (a duty to the ICO), tell the public generally (reputational and practical), and tell the specific people at high risk directly (a duty to them, triggered by risk severity, discharged by neither of the other two). Gated on possession of affected_data_schedule (evidence obtained at m040/m041b) rather than the bare exfil_confirmed flag — the duty is triggered by actually knowing who's at risk, not just suspecting someone is.

routes.label: Notify the ICO now — still within the statutory window
routes.stakes: The statement already went to residents and press before the ICO heard anything from you directly — an unusual sequence, and one a post-incident review will ask about — but the statutory duty is notification within seventy-two hours, not notification first in some fixed order, and this call is comfortably inside that window.
routes.hook: Someone finally asks the question out loud: did anyone actually call the regulator? The silence that follows answers it — but there's still plenty of clock left to fix that.

narrative.player_intent: Complete the statutory notification, out of the usual order but well within time.
narrative.dramatic_role: The recovery route out of the ICO-skip: only visible while regulator_notified is still false. Deliberately does not cost exposure or imply a breach — ICO-before-press is not a legal requirement, only a sequencing convention, and penalising it here would teach the wrong lesson. Routes through regulator_call itself (via the existing m090 loop back to public_disclosure) so the reference number and NCSC contact still land, just in an unusual order.

routes.label: Refuse to pay, restore from backup
routes.foreshadow: The recovery panel won't sign off on a restore attempt without a verified backup status in hand — nobody gets to gamble on a diagram's word alone. And the insurer has noticed something else: there's no ICO reference number on this file. A clean recovery doesn't cover a missed statutory duty.
routes.stakes: Refusing the ransom bets everything on the backups actually being clean. The verified status report says segmentation held for most of the estate, not all of it — a genuine gamble even with the evidence in hand, not a formality.
routes.hook: The backups were segmented on the diagram. The report in your hand says: mostly.

narrative.player_intent: Refuse the ransom and recover independently.
narrative.dramatic_role: The clean-recovery finale gamble; the one moment fortune, not judgement, decides the outcome. Gated on evidence (backup_status_report, from m045) rather than the attacker's countdown — restoration viability depends on what the recovery team actually verified, not on how much of the ransom deadline happens to be left. The reckless m011 branch never visits forensics_engage, so it never obtains this report either; losing access to both the integrity comms route and the clean-recovery ending is a deliberate, consistent consequence of that branch, not a separate oversight.

routes.label: Authorise payment and pursue the decryptor
routes.stakes: A quiet payment buys speed and certainty, creates a paper trail of its own, and — the insurer was clear about this part — leaves the bill uncovered. It does not buy a guarantee: NCSC guidance is explicit that paying carries no assurance the criminals hand over a working decryptor, or hand over anything at all.
routes.hook: Quiet is doing a lot of work in 'quiet payment' — and 'paid' is not the same word as 'solved'.

narrative.player_intent: Pay the ransom against the insurer's explicit advice.
narrative.dramatic_role: The mixed-outcome finale road, now genuinely a gamble rather than a guaranteed purchase — reflecting NCSC's guidance that ransomware payment carries no guarantee of recovery. Failure lands you paying and still losing everything, the worst combined outcome.

routes.label: Move to public disclosure
routes.stakes: The regulator knows. Residents and press are next.
routes.hook: A notification logged with the ICO is not the same as a notification the public has seen.

narrative.player_intent: Proceed to public disclosure after notifying the regulator.
narrative.dramatic_role: Hub spoke to the public disclosure endgame.

routes.label: Assess the damage
routes.stakes: There is no version of this assessment that comes back small.
routes.hook: The backups are gone. There is nothing left to contain.

narrative.player_intent: Face the full scope of the overrun.
narrative.dramatic_role: Route into the catastrophic ending.

routes.label: Face the fallout
routes.stakes: The story has already turned. There is no statement left that changes that.
routes.hook: The FOI response is due, and there is no answer that doesn't confirm what the journalist already suspects.

narrative.player_intent: Confront the consequences of the exposed cover-up.
narrative.dramatic_role: Route into the scandal ending. The condition is always true in practice — cover_up_exposed is only ever reached after a route that sets cover_up_attempted — but stating it lets validation.ending_assertions prove ending_scandal's prose is honest, since the static checker only looks one hop back.

validation.notes: Counts are maintained by scripts/validate-adventure.js and the CI reachability gate; see README.
